description_long: This is a compact, all-black submachine gun with a minimalist, utilitarian design reminiscent of the British Sten gun. The rifle features a cylindrical receiver and barrel assembly, a simple tubular stock with a circular buttplate, and a side-mounted detachable box magazine. The trigger guard is angular and the grip is integrated into the lower receiver, while the magazine well protrudes horizontally from the left side. The barrel is thin and extends directly from the receiver, with no visible muzzle device. Iron sights are mounted directly on the receiver and barrel, providing basic aiming capability. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal, with a matte finish and minimal embellishments, suitable for rugged urban use.
description: A compact, all-black submachine gun with a tubular stock, side-mounted magazine, and minimalist iron sights. Its utilitarian design is reminiscent of the British Sten gun.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical barrel, circular buttplate, and the side-mounted magazine protruding to the left.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the tubular stock, side-mounted magazine, and the simple iron sights along the top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the circular buttplate, straight tubular stock, and the magazine extending to the right.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the magazine well, trigger guard, and the full length of the barrel and stock.
